helpful: Full petition text with the appended Fourth Circuit opinion and district-court order made the vehicle analysis (independent alternative holding) possible without any live retrieval.
statpack.json per-fee-class per-Term grant rates gave a much better anchor (paid ~5.4%) than the whole-docket ~3% rate.
The relist-count cut plus sampled granted priors' distribution_count field gave a concrete zero-relist down-weight.
gaps: The prompt references a 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack section, but the committed statpack.md/statpack.json carries no such table; I anchored on the paid fee-class rate instead.
No free-text or subject-matter filter on `fedcourts query` for SCOTUS rows (--topic is circuit-only), so I could not pull priors for similar Rehabilitation Act / interactive-process petitions.

A response-waiver / call-for-response signal cut in the statpack would be valuable: SG waiver with no CFR is one of the strongest deny signals on a live docket and currently has no quantified base rate.
notes: Forward-mode paid petition distributed to the long conference on a waived response; prediction rests mainly on provisioned inputs plus committed base rates.
